title: Race-lap ranking specialist blended with probability model
summary: Public ranking docs show CatBoost and LightGBM can optimize within-group ordering.
rationale: Public ranking docs show CatBoost and LightGBM can optimize within-group ordering. Pit decisions are inherently relative: on a given race lap, only some cars are truly in the stop window. A ranking specialist can learn who is most likely to pit among peers even if absolute probabilities are mis-scaled.
implementation_hint: Train a separate model with groups such as Race-Year-LapNumber or Race-Year-LapNumber-CompoundRegime using CatBoost PairLogit/QueryCrossEntropy or LightGBM lambdarank; evaluate QueryAUC in grouped CV; blend its OOF scores with the main binary model, then calibrate the blend back to probabilities.
expected_effect: Improves pairwise ordering inside pit waves and may convert similar CV into better public AUC when pit events cluster by lap.
risk: Groups with very few positives can make ranking unstable. Ranking scores are not calibrated, so direct submission without blending/calibration is risky.
